summary: The frozen release candidate preserves the production runtime contract: guarded one-command closure advances only through real managed-worktree HEAD changes, typed resumable stops, independent needs_changes repair and re-review, and final current-HEAD Gate rebinding. PR creation, merge, waiver, and material external effects remain explicit human operations.
inspection_inputs: node --test --test-name-pattern='same HEAD|needs_changes repair|final_prepare|material ambiguity|provider fallback|cancel|legacy profile' test/one-command-pr-ready-closure.test.js test/guarded-run-session.test.js test/independent-review-orchestrator.test.js
inspection_summary: Inspected the exact-HEAD production owner composition and public CLI route; managed-worktree and authoritative Git HEAD validation; provider/auth/quota/permission/timeout/cancellation typed-stop contracts; persisted status/resume/cancel and legacy migration paths; independent read-only review lifecycle; bounded needs_changes repair with verify/review suffix replay; final_prepare current-HEAD Gate enforcement; and the absence of autonomous PR-create, merge, waiver, deploy, publish, or other material external-effect nodes. Verified that the Story and architecture compose rather than duplicate the already-merged Production Runtime Connectors (PR #377) and Independent Review Orchestration (PR #382). Reviewed fresh exact-HEAD unit, E2E, integration, typecheck, and conformance artifacts and independently reran 16 focused runtime-contract tests, all passing. No API, DB, auth schema, UI, or network protocol migration is introduced; environment/provider failures remain typed and recoverable.
judgment_delta: Initial concern that runtime-reported completion could falsely advance the Run -> resolved because implementation and repair require a real managed-worktree HEAD advance and reject same-HEAD or reported-versus-authoritative-HEAD mismatch.

Initial concern that restart, human decision, provider failure, timeout, or cancellation could lose authority -> resolved by persisted typed stop state, exact resume cursors, bounded Human Decision descriptors, ordered provider fallback, and cancellation authority winning stale runtime completion.

Initial concern that needs_changes could be treated as success or repaired without independent re-review -> resolved because findings are preserved, repair advances HEAD, and the verify/review/final_prepare suffix is invalidated and replayed with a convergence bound.

Initial concern that stale evidence could yield pr_ready -> resolved because final_prepare re-runs PR preparation, requires the prepared artifact to match authoritative current HEAD, and fails closed as gate_recheck_required or ci_pending when readiness is absent.

Initial concern that the final Story might reimplement PR #377 or PR #382 or broaden external authority -> resolved because production connectors and independent review remain their existing owners, while the autonomous DAG contains no PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, or other material external-effect action.

Regression guard and path-surface coverage concern -> resolved by public CLI run/status/resume/cancel, managed-worktree E2E, legacy and fallback tests, generated Gate artifact behavior, exact-HEAD 288/288 unit evidence, 4/4 E2E evidence, 4/4 integration evidence, typecheck, improving conformance 81 to 80, and an independent 16/16 focused rerun whose assertions would fail on same-HEAD acceptance, stale final preparation, skipped repair replay, forged readiness, or lost typed stops.
